location.placeName: J ONE, Marasi Drive, Business Bay

entries.title: Fog of War
entries.content:
Today was supposed to be our final day in the hotel. We had just picked up the keys to our new apartment, and the plan was to go on a nice double date with a pair of friends. Afterwards, we were going to buy furniture so we could finally settle down after four months of hotel living.

However, the world seemed to have another plan.

While we were mid romantic boat ride with our friends, the news started rolling in that the U.S. and Israel had hit Iran, and that Iran was retaliating by firing toward the Gulf. What was supposed to be a romantic boat ride quickly turned into a panicked one—taking phone calls and trying to figure out what this meant for us, but also for our employees on the ground.

Once the boat docked, we headed back to our hotel room and invited our coworkers over so none of us had to experience the chaos alone. We ordered some shawarma and sat together, hanging out in confusion.

I found myself feeling a mixture of anxiety, excitement but also fun. I would be lying if I said the novelty seeker in me was not a bit fascinated but ultimately I was concerned for what this means. I hope this will be a 48 hour ordeal that does not really impact Dubai.
entries.newsHeadline: US-Israeli strikes kill Khamenei and Iranian retaliation shakes Gulf - As it happened

location.placeName: Port de La Mer, Street 117, Jumeirah

entries.title: War or Not I am Moving In!
entries.content:
I already paid a whole year’s rent upfront because that’s basically how you do it in Dubai. So yes, maybe my furniture shopping got derailed, but I won’t let this war stop me from moving into my apartment!!

So, against the missile shelter advice, we Ubered this morning to our new apartment and picked up the keys. Now that we have the apartment, I feel a bit more annoyed, since it’s starting to settle in that we might not ACTUALLY be able to move in soon—and that this era of hotel living might have to continue for longer.

This is amplified by the fact that, as we explored our new neighbourhood, we completely fell in love. We even found an incredible restaurant that allows dogs, right by the beach, and the food was amazing.

As I ate this incredible duck pizza by the water, you could hear the fighter jets flying by the port and the occasional interception explosion. There was something very surreal about this contrast—being in this awesome, very peaceful-feeling neighbourhood, but hearing war in the background.

It made me realize that when I studied history, I always thought of war as something constant, as opposed to a collection of events distributed both temporally and spatially. Even during war, life goes on.

For safety reasons, we decided to rent a car—the Jetour T2! I’m pretty excited about it since it looks really cool, and we’re even thinking of buying the same car. We’ll use it to drive to Al Ain and camp out with Hina’s parents for a bit, since Al Ain is far from the action.
entries.newsHeadline: Missile debris, panic buying jolt Dubai's aura of calm after Iran attack

location.placeName: Al Ain Zoo, Ar Ruqiy Street, Falaj Hazza'

entries.title: The old stomping grounds
entries.content:
In a weird twist of fate, the safest place in the GCC right now seems to be my old hometown of Al Ain. So we’ve been hiding out here for a few days, staying with Hina’s family. Al Ain is so far removed from the action that when we arrived, Hina’s parents didn’t even know anything was going on.

Other than the occasional middle-of-the-night missile alarm that wakes you up and ruins your sleep, you wouldn’t be able to tell anything is happening…

Funnily enough, I’ve learned something new about myself during this stay: I hate being bored so much that I’d rather get bombed in Dubai than be safe and bored in Al Ain… how did I survive five years in this town?!

The boredom has gotten so bad that I’m spending every non-working waking minute glued to the Monitor Situation app, which has done nothing but skyrocket my anxiety. As a result, we’ve started weighing our options regarding evacuation.
entries.newsHeadline: Gulf Nations’ Stockpiles of Interceptors Dwindle Amid Iran’s Relentless Drone and Missile Attacks

location.placeName: Consulate of the United States of America, Al Seef Street, Bur Dubai

entries.title: Closing deals mid bomb
entries.content:
So… we’re back in Dubai which, honestly, has brought me back to life. Turns out boredom was hitting my mental health harder than I thought.

What’s wild is that despite the US consulate in Dubai being a major target of attacks last night, I still had a pretty important in-person meeting today LITERALLY a block away from it.

And somehow… the meeting went great.

This is definitely going down as one of the spiciest work stories I’ll ever have. All things considered, today was a good day.
entries.newsHeadline: Fire breaks out near US consulate in Dubai after drone strike

location.placeName: Al Ain Zoo, Khor Al Mighayra Street, Falaj Hazza'

entries.title: Back to Al Ain
entries.content:
Things have started to pick up in Dubai and all of our flights keep getting canceled and we cant seem to find any real evacuation options that are dog friendly. Feeling a bit defeated but for safety reasons we have moved back to Al Ain for now.

Also on a separate note... the Jetour T2 SUCKS! I have completely lost faith in Chinese cars. I guess American tech and innovation is safe after all...
entries.newsHeadline: Trump says no deal with Iran until ‘unconditional surrender’

location.placeName: Zayed International Airport, Sheikh Khalifa Bin Zayed Street, Zayed International Airport

entries.title: Last minute evac...
entries.content:
While I was 2 hours into an 8 hour off site SF time, at 10 pm Al Ain time we get a call letting us know that there is a flight out of Abu Dhabi at 3am to Milan that we can get on with the dog! We obviously accepted this window immediately turned off the meeting got dressed and drove to Abu Dhabi Airport. At the airport we actually had to evacuate TWICE! due to missile alerts while we where waiting for take of.. it was pretty bizarre. 

Once we got on the plan we where advised to NOT put our seatbelts on until its time for take off just incase we have to last minute evac... Luckily we managed to take off safely and despite the HORRIBLE turbulence due to a VERY out of character for the UAE thunderstorm we are safely in route to Milan.
entries.newsHeadline: Trump Denies U.S. Responsibility for Bombing of Girls’ School That Killed 175 in Iran

entries.title: Escaped WW3?
entries.content: Early this morning we arrived in Milan after a LOONG night of travel. We are safe and celebrated checking into our hotel by taking a free photo booth photo. Feeling a bit relieved to have made it out safely after all the failed attempts. Not sure what our plan is yet but we can stay in the EU for 80ish days before we have to find somewhere else to be. Going to try to enjoy some good food and find a way to distract ourselves form this temporary displacment.
entries.newsHeadline: Iran Says U.S. and Israeli Strikes Have Killed 1,348 Civilians

entries.title: ROMA!
entries.content: In 2015 I was briefly homeless and in that time I moved to Rome while I figured my shit out. We took the train down from Milan this morning and history repeats itself as 11 years later I find myself displaced and taking shelter in Roma once more!
entries.newsHeadline: Trump urges countries to send warships to help secure Strait of Hormuz

location.placeName: Via Ennio Quirino Visconti, Prati, Municipio Roma I

entries.title: A Gazillion Dollars!
entries.content:
It’s been almost a full work week in Rome, and honestly, it feels confusing.

I wake up stressed. We still don’t know where we’ll be in three days. We have a rare vacation coming up, but instead of excitement, I’m dreading it—it feels like a waste of time off when we’ll probably spend it figuring out logistics instead of actually enjoying ourselves.

Then the day starts: back-to-back meetings, crammed into a small hotel room with no proper desk. I’m hunched over, my back hurting, trying to operate as if this is normal.

And then work ends.

I step outside, start walking, and it hits me—I’m in Rome.

The architecture, the light, the musicians playing near these absurdly beautiful landmarks… and suddenly I’m aware of how strange this all is. I’m oscillating between the stress of being displaced by WW3 and this deep, almost guilty gratitude that this displacement has turned into something that looks a lot like a vacation.

It reminds me of something Kamran said when he saw how our “escape WW3” plan turned into an impromptu Roman getaway:
“It’s funny what a gazillion dollars can do to a tough situation.”

He’s right.

Throw enough money at a problem and it doesn’t just become manageable—it can start to feel like it isn’t a problem at all.

That’s exactly what this is.

We’ve thrown enough money at the situation that it’s become… almost enjoyable. And that realization—more than anything else—makes me feel incredibly grateful for the position I’m in.
entries.newsHeadline: Iran war live: Tehran confirms Larijani, Soleimani killed

location.placeName: Ponte Cavour, Campo Marzio, Municipio Roma I

entries.title: Bad burgers in Rome
entries.content:
Fun fact about me, I don’t actually like McDonald’s. Yet, despite being in Rome one of the food capitals of the world while equipped with an adult credit card that allows me to buy any food I want—I find myself eating an unhealthy $4 cheeseburger from McDonald’s that I don’t even enjoy. 

I found myself wondering why that is. I think the answer is that I’m craving familiarity. In a time of moving, uncertainty, and not knowing where we’re going to live, even something I don’t really like can feel comforting due to its predictability. It’s a small taste of something familiar—a way to emulate routine and patterns while everything else is changing.
entries.newsHeadline: 'Extensive damage' at Qatar industrial site as Iran retaliates for strike on gas field

location.placeName: Via Leonardo da Vinci, Fiumicino, Roma Capitale

entries.title: Gaslighting myself to Vacation
entries.content: About to head to my two favorite cities in Europe for Eid vacation yet I feel very unexcited. In the middle of all this chaos and forced travel taking a break to pleasure travel feels a bit absurd like we are trying to gaslight ourselves into pretending this whole thing is a vacation. Alas… we shall try to actually get some R&R and make the most of this trip.
entries.newsHeadline: Iran war escalates, energy prices spike after Israeli strike on South Pars gas field

location.placeName: Marriott Hotel, 4, Apáczai Csere János utca

entries.title: Vacation A New Style
entries.content:
First day of vacation is mostly a success thanks to taking a longer than usual nap! Given the circumstances I’m not engaging in the usual style oh high energy high exploration vacationing I like and focusing on just chilling.

I’m also realizing that lack of routine is starting to destroy my physical health. So as per Trevor’s suggestions I’m going to start simple and just do 25 burpees every second day and see what happens
entries.newsHeadline: Supreme leader denounces US-Israel ‘delusion’ of domination

entries.title: Vienna waits for you
entries.content:
Slow down, you crazy child
You're so ambitious for a juvenile
But then if you're so smart
Tell me why are you still so afraid? Mm
Where's the fire, what's the hurry about?
You'd better cool it off before you burn it out
You've got so much to do
And only so many hours in a day, hey

But you know that when the truth is told
That you can get what you want or you can just get old
You're gonna kick off before you even get halfway through, ooh
When will you realize Vienna waits for you?

Slow down, you're doing fine
You can't be everything you wanna be before your time
Although it's so romantic on the borderline tonight, tonight
Too bad, but it's the life you lead
You're so ahead of yourself, that you forgot what you need
Though you can see when you're wrong
You know you can't always see when you're right
You're right

You've got your passion, you've got your pride
But don't you know that only fools are satisfied?
Dream on, but don't imagine they'll all come true, ooh
When will you realize Vienna waits for you?

Slow down, you crazy child
And take the phone off the hook and disappear for a while
It's alright, you can afford to lose a day or two, ooh
When will you realize Vienna waits for you?

And you know that when the truth is told
That you can get what you want or you could just get old
You're gonna kick off before you even get halfway through, ooh
Why don't you realize Vienna waits for you?
When will you realize Vienna waits for you?
entries.newsHeadline: Iran Threatens Attacks on Commercial Ships in Bab al-Mandeb Strait

entries.title: Vienna is Pure Livable Magic
entries.content:
After arriving in Vienna and going for a beautiful evening walk I feel so extremely relaxed. This city is somehow even better than I remember it, walking through the streets at night had such a calming effect that simply feels unreal. 

I would very much love to permanently relocate here and can see it being such a huge net positive for my life. Unfortunately there is no easy way to do so.

I’m confident that Vienna is the greatest city in Europe and the only city other than Tokyo with a real chance in a head to head against NYC!
entries.newsHeadline: Trump says US will ‘obliterate’ Iran’s power plants if Strait of Hormuz not open before 48-hour deadline
